text.title: docker Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for docker as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using the latest tag for base images or pushed images.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: latest is mutable — a base image update can change behavior without any change to the Dockerfile. Pin specific versions (python:3.12.7-slim) for reproducible builds.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Root inside container maps to root on host if namespace isolation is bypassed. Create a non-root user in the Dockerfile and set USER before the final CMD.

failure-mode.description: Storing secrets in ENV instructions or build ARGs.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: ENV values are visible in docker inspect, image history, and any process that reads /proc/self/environ. Use Docker secrets, runtime environment injection, or an external vault at container startup.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without HEALTHCHECK, orchestrators (Kubernetes, ECS, Swarm) cannot distinguish a running-but-broken container from a healthy one. Containers stuck in crash-loops with no readiness signals cause silent traffic loss.
